sections.paragraph:
Chald. Ithpa. to conquer, to excel followed by עַל Dan 6:4. (Syriac id.).

I. נֵצַח & נֶצַח with suff. נִצְחִי.

(1) splendour, glory. 1Ch 29:11 1Sa 15:29, נֵצַח יִשְׂרָאֵל “the glory of Israel” (i.e. God).

(2) sincerity, truth. Hab 1:4, לֹא יֵצֵא לָנֶצַח מִשְׁפָּט “judgment goeth not forth according to truth;” perhaps, Pro 21:28.

(3) faith, confidence. Lam 3:18, אָבַד נִצְחִי “my confidence is perished.”

(4) perpetuity (as we can confide in those things which endure, which are perpetual, compare נֶאֱמָן No. 3, 4), eternity, for ever. עַד נֵצַח Psa 49:20 Job 34:36 נֶצַח, לָנֶצַח adv. perpetually. Isa 34:10, לְנֵצַח נְצָחִים id.

(5) perfection, completeness, acc. נֶצַח and לָנֶצַח adv. altogether, quite (comp. Germ. lauter). Psa 13:2, עַד אָנָה יְהֹוָה תִּשְׁכָּחֵנִי נֶצַח “how long, O Lord, wilt thou altogether forget me?” Psa 79:5 Job 23:7. In genit., Psa 74:3, מַשּׁוּאֹות נֵצַח “complete desolations,” i.e. places altogether, absolutely desolated. [In Thes. this signification is almost rejected, and these passages are taken as in the signification of No. 4.]

II. נֵצַח m. juice, liquor, as scattered from grapes when trodden in the press, Isa 63:3, Isa 63:6from the root נָצַח No. II.
